title: Build an Image From a Dockerfile
description:
## Build a Tagged Image

A build context has been prepared for you at **`/root/dockerlab`** containing a `Dockerfile`:

```dockerfile
FROM alpine:latest
RUN echo "built in kubekosh" > /msg.txt
CMD ["cat", "/msg.txt"]
```

**Requirements:**
1. Build an image from that Dockerfile
2. Tag it as **`myapp:1.0`**

> 💡 `docker build -t <name>:<tag> <context>`. The context is the directory containing the Dockerfile — run the build from inside `/root/dockerlab` and use `.` as the context.

hints.title: Build with a tag
hints.body: cd into the build context, then build with -t to set the repository and tag.

hints.title: Verify the image and its output
hints.body: List the image, then run it once to confirm the CMD works (it should print the message).
hints.command: docker images myapp && docker run --rm myapp:1.0

validation.description: Checks that an image tagged myapp:1.0 was built and runs the expected command.

commands.description: Running the image prints the build message
